summary:
The baby has not shown any tooth eruption by 10 months which indicates possible developmental delays; further evaluation and possibly referral should occur as per standard protocols.
The absence of deciduous tooth eruption at 10 months suggests delayed development necessitating diagnostic workup or specialist consultation according to established guidelines. \boxed{} Agent1’s summary focuses primarily on identifying when dental eruptions typically start and noting that no eruption by ten months may indicate issues needing attention:

**Key Points Summarized:**  
No primary teeth have appeared by 10 months post-birth, indicating potentially abnormal development warranting further assessment. 

This aligns closely while condensing into two succinct statements focusing directly on critical aspects related to diagnosis and next steps based upon observed milestones being missed. 
\boxed{}

To summarize more concisely yet comprehensively within given constraints,

By 10 months, lack of tooth eruption warrants concern suggesting underlying developmental issue likely needs professional examination promptly.\boxed{}
